original_text:
### Contrast
- **Test contrast levels** (webaim.com or equivalent) for every text/background pair. [GENERAL minimums: WCAG AA — 4.5:1 body text, 3:1 large text ≥24px or 19px bold.]
- Known-safe pairs: Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, Graphite on White/Light Blue/Golden Sand; White on Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy. **Avoid:** Gold or Sunshine text on White (fails contrast) — golds are for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.
- **Don't rely on color alone** to convey meaning — pair with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.

relations.note: Refines the specific restriction on using gold/sunshine text.

quotes: GENERAL minimums: WCAG AA — 4.5:1 body text, 3:1 large text ≥24px or 19px bold.

rules.intent: Maintain regulatory accessibility standards across all brand emails.
rules.rule_text: All text and background pairs must meet WCAG AA contrast standards, requiring a minimum ratio of 4.5:1 for body text and 3:1 for large text (24px or larger, or 19px bold). Ensure compliance using tools like webaim.com.

quotes: Known-safe pairs: Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, Graphite on White/Light Blue/Golden Sand; White on Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy.
Avoid: Gold or Sunshine text on White (fails contrast) — golds are for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.

rules.intent: Ensure text legibility and contrast compliance by enforcing approved background/foreground pairs.
rules.rule_text: Use only known-safe contrast color pairs. Approved combinations include Brand Blue (#00529b), Deep Blue (#003D74), Dark Navy (#011E45), or Graphite (#212121) text on White (#ffffff), Light Blue (#E6F0F9), or Golden Sand (#FEF1C8) backgrounds. White (#ffffff) text is approved on Brand Blue (#00529b), Deep Blue (#003D74), and Dark Navy (#011E45) backgrounds. Never use Gold (#ffc60a) or Sunshine (#faa31b) text on White (#ffffff).

quotes: Avoid: Gold or Sunshine text on White (fails contrast) — golds are for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.

rules.intent: Prevent unreadable text treatments and preserve the role of yellow accents.
rules.rule_text: Do not use Gold (#ffc60a) or Sunshine (#faa31b) for regular body or paragraph text. These colors are reserved strictly for surfaces, horizontal rules, and large display emphasis.

quotes: Don't rely on color alone to convey meaning — pair with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.

rules.intent: Support color-blind and visually impaired users by eliminating single-channel communication.
rules.rule_text: Do not rely on color alone to convey meaning or hierarchy. Information delivered via color cues must be paired with secondary channels, such as icons, visual depth, explicit copy, or interactive visual feedback.
